other_observers: Kelly Shipe and Jeannie Mitchell on the first date, many others later on.; We found this bird. It was seen by many people through October 24, 2011
duration: Off and for nearly two hours
specific_location: Pueblo Reservoir State Wildlife Area, west end of Pueblo Reservoir.
description: I was on a house boat at Pueblo Reservoir with birders Kelly Shipe and Jeannie Mitchell when I spotted a gull like bird on the water ahead of us. I asked the captain to head toward the bird slowly so we could look at it. When we got closer I thought the bird was probably a jaeger. Closer looks revealed a whitish head, smallish bill, and gray tones to the back, all of which made me think the bird was a Long-tailed Jaeger. See photos from the first date from the boat.Behaviors: Swimming and flying around on the large Pueblo Reservoir.Call: nonePlumage: light morph juvenile
photographer: Photographer: Brandon K Percival, Brandon K Percival, Brandon K Percival, Brandon K Percival, Brandon K Percival
